because.text: Codex ran the whole executable surface incl. all four Lean bridges - no P0, all three Books agree across Python/Rust/Go. P1 (accepted): eval_hash checked max_* limits only at steps%256, so a term normalizing before step 256 could return over its configured max_node_depth/max_materialized_nodes (FALSE at depth-limit 1 returned depth 2) - ResourceFault contract violated (not a canonical split; faults are local s3.6). Fixed with a resource_check() helper on the 256-sample AND before every normal-form return; 4 regressions pinned (over-depth/over-size fault, exact-bound returns, Omega/depth-1500 unchanged); 49 vectors byte-/ATP-identical. Rust has no configurable limits so the controls are Python-oracle-local (documented). P2 (accepted): tools/test-all.sh gives the full matrix one command. Verified locally on the MacBook (compute local, secrets remote).

subject.note: Codex v0.6.4 runtime hardening audit: no P0, P1 resource-limit fix + P2 test-all
